author: Dr. Lizard
title: When should danicopan be added to C5 inhibitor therapy in PNH?
answer: Danicopan is added when patients develop symptomatic anemia from extravascular hemolysis despite ongoing C5 inhibitor treatment.
detail: Danicopan is a factor D inhibitor approved by FDA in 2024 as add-on therapy to eculizumab or ravulizumab.
It blocks C3 convertase formation, reducing C3 fragment deposition and subsequent macrophage-mediated red cell clearance.
The combination maintains C5 blockade for intravascular hemolysis while adding proximal inhibition for extravascular hemolysis control.
This dual approach avoids the breakthrough hemolysis risk of switching from C5 inhibitor to C3 inhibitor monotherapy.
releaseNote: Danicopan is added to C5 inhibitor therapy in PNH when patients develop symptomatic anemia from extravascular hemolysis, providing a dual approach to hemolysis control while avoiding breakthrough hemolysis risk.
